meta.title: Credit Note vs Invoice: How to Fix Billing Mistakes
meta.description: Learn what a credit note (credit memo) is and how it differs from an invoice. Discover the legally compliant way to correct overbilling mistakes.

blocks.title: Credit Note vs. Invoice
blocks.subtitle: Mistakes happen. If you accidentally overcharge a client, you cannot simply delete the invoice. Learn how to use Credit Notes to fix billing errors legally.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: The Opposite of an Invoice
blocks.text: An invoice asks a client to pay you money. A credit note (or credit memo) does the exact opposite: it acknowledges that you owe the client money, or that they owe you less than originally stated. In professional accounting, once an invoice is finalized and sent, it becomes a permanent record. You cannot just delete it. If the amount was wrong, you must issue a credit note to legally cancel out the incorrect amount.

blocks.definition: A commercial document issued by a seller to a buyer stating that a certain amount has been credited to the buyer's account, effectively lowering their invoice balance.

blocks.title: Credit Note vs Invoice
rows: Increases the amount the buyer owes
Decreases the amount the buyer owes
When you sell a product or service
When goods are returned, or you overcharged

steps.title: Identify the Original Invoice
steps.description: Every credit note must explicitly reference the original invoice number it is correcting. (e.g., 'Credit applied to Invoice #104').

steps.title: Format as a Negative Amount
steps.description: The line items on a credit note should reflect the exact items being returned or discounted, resulting in a negative total balance.

steps.title: Reverse the Tax
steps.description: If you charged sales tax or VAT on the original invoice, the credit note must also reverse that exact tax amount so you don't overpay the government.

blocks.tip: If a client has already paid the incorrect invoice in full, a credit note can either be refunded directly to their bank account, or kept on file to offset the cost of their next future invoice.

questions.question: Is a credit note the same as a refund?
questions.description: Not exactly. A refund is the actual physical transfer of cash back to the buyer. A credit note is the accounting document that justifies why that cash was transferred, or why the client's next bill will be cheaper.

blocks.heading: Need to issue a credit note?
blocks.subtext: You can use our free generator. Just change the title to 'Credit Note' and enter negative quantities for your line items.
